tldr: Replace Ably pub/sub with Stream's Chat or Feeds APIs when your real-time use case is primarily messaging or activity feeds.
why_switch: Higher-level chat and feed primitives instead of raw pub/sub
Strong SDK coverage for web, iOS, Android, React Native
Activity feed aggregation out of the box
Single vendor for chat plus feeds
what_to_export: Message history (if persisted in your DB)
Moderation rules currently enforced in app code
export_method: Inventory your Ably channels and any message history you store yourself. If you keep messages in your own database, prepare them for import via Stream's server SDK. For ephemeral pub/sub state, no export is needed; you re-establish subscriptions on the new client.
import_method: Create a Stream app, install the Chat or Feeds SDK on clients, and map your existing channels/rooms to Stream channels. Use Stream's server SDK to bulk-import historical messages and users if required. Run both stacks in parallel during cutover and migrate user cohorts incrementally.

concept_mapping.note: Stream channels carry message history natively
Use Stream's WebSocket SDK; no MQTT bridge

gotchas: Stream is opinionated around chat/feeds; pure pub/sub fits less cleanly
Pricing tier jumps can be steep for high MAU apps
Message persistence semantics differ from Ably's ephemeral pub/sub
Moderation features may overlap with custom logic you already wrote
Plan for user identity migration and token rotation

email.subject: Cancelling Ably subscription
email.body:
Hi Ably team,

We're moving our real-time chat and feeds workload to Stream. Please confirm the cancellation date and any final invoice.

Thanks.
